text: Git branching is a key part of version control that helps teams work on code without causing conflicts. A common workflow involves creating feature branches. When you start a new task, you create a separate branch from the main codebase. This allows you to make changes safely without affecting the main project. Once your feature is complete, you can merge it back into the main branch. In many teams, this is done through a pull request. A pull request lets other developers review your changes before they are merged. This process helps catch errors and ensures code quality. Overall, branching makes collaboration smoother and keeps your project organized.
